questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: Which of the following processes is most suitable for extracting sodium chloride from sea water?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: Neon exists as a gas at room temperature and pressure because

options.label: C
options.text: the attractive force between neon atoms is weak.

options.label: D
options.text: the outermost electron shell of a neon atom has an octet structure.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: A certain mass of a sample of  \( Ag_{2}O(s) \)  is strongly heated in a test tube. Which of the following shows the relationship of the mass of the contents (m) in the test tube with time (t) from the start of heating?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
If 8.0 g of sulphur dioxide gas contains n molecules, how many molecules does 2.0 g of oxygen gas contain?

(Relative atomic masses : O = 16.0, S = 32.0)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: Quartz \(SiO_{2}\) is harder than dry ice \(CO_{2}\) because

options.label: A
options.text: the atomic size of silicon is larger than that of carbon.

options.label: B
options.text: a silicon atom has more electrons than a carbon atom has.

options.label: C
options.text: quartz has a giant network structure, but dry ice consists of discrete molecules.

options.label: D
options.text: the silicon–oxygen bond in quartz is strong, but the carbon–oxygen bond in dry ice is weak.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Dilute sodium hydroxide solution is added to a 0.1 M solution until in excess. Which of the following combinations is correct?
Solution    |    Observation

options.label: D
options.text: iron(III) sulphate    |    dirty green precipitate formed

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: Which of the following statements concerning iron and magnesium is correct?

options.label: A
options.text: Iron is ductile but magnesium is not.

options.label: B
options.text: Iron corrodes less readily than magnesium.

options.label: C
options.text: The abundance of magnesium is higher than that of iron in the earth crust.

options.label: D
options.text: Both magnesium and iron can have more than one oxidation number in their oxides.

questions.question_text: Which of the following molecular formulae can represent an alkanoic acid?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: X, Y and Z are different metals. When they are placed separately in NaCl(aq), only Y gives colourless gas bubbles. When each of their oxides is heated strongly, only the oxide of X gives a colourless gas. Which of the following shows the decreasing order of reactivity of these three metals?

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 7: Redox Reactions, Chemical Cells and Electrolysis
questions.question_text: Which of the following reagents does NOT react with copper?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Consider the solutions W, X, Y and Z below :
W100 cm³ of 0.20 M HNO₃(aq)
X50 cm³ of 0.20 M HCl(aq)
Y100 cm³ of 0.20 M CH₃CO₂H(aq)
Z50 cm³ of 0.10 M NaOH(aq)
Which of the following statements is correct?

options.label: B
options.text: Mixing W and Z gives a neutral solution.

options.label: C
options.text: The pH of the mixture of W and X is lower than that of W.

options.label: D
options.text: The pH of the mixture of W and X is lower than that of the mixture of X and Y.

(1) \(BCl_{3}\)
(2) \(PCl_{3}\)
(3)  \( CHCl_{3} \)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Which of the following statements is / are correct?

(1) The density of  \( H_{2}O(l) \)  is lower than that of  \( H_{2}O(g) \) .
(2) When ice changes to water, the open structure of ice collapses.
(3) When the temperature of water rises from  \( 10°C \)  to  \( 30°C \) , the average distance between  \( H_{2}O \)  molecules increases.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Consider the following information :
\( 2H_{2}O(l)\rightarrow2H_{2}(g)+O_{2}(g) \)    \( \Delta H^{\theta}=+x\ kJ\ mol^{-1} \)
(1) The standard enthalpy change of formation of  \( H_{2}O(l) \)  is  \( -0.5x \)  kJ mol \( ^{-1} \) .
(2) The standard enthalpy change of formation of  \( H_{2}O(l) \)  i \( +0.5x \)  kJ mol \( ^{-1} \) .
(3) The standard enthalpy change of combustion of  \( H_{2}(g) \)  is  \( -x \)  kJ mol \( ^{-1} \) .

Which of the following statements is / are correct?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
In an experiment, marble is heated in a boiling tube and the gas evolved is passed into a test tube with limewater. Which of the following statements concerning the experiment is / are correct?

(1) The marble turns brick red upon heating.
(2) The limewater turns milky initially but eventually becomes a colourless solution.
(3) If marble is replaced by chalk, a similar observation would be obtained.

questions.question_text:
Which of the following statements concerning a zinc-carbon cell is / are INCORRECT?

(1) The graphite rod is inserted in a mixture of graphite powder and  \( Mn_{2}O_{3} \) .
(2) Potassium hydroxide acts as an electrolyte.
(3) Ammonia forms around the cathode.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Which of the following processes are endothermic?

(1) melting of wax
(2) cracking of heavy oil
(3) adding zinc powder to  \( CuSO_{4}(aq) \)

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 7: Redox Reactions, Chemical Cells and Electrolysis
questions.question_text:
Consider the following chemical cell :
[IMG:0.jpg]
Which of the following statements are correct?

(1) The pH of the solution in compartment A decreases gradually.
(2) Hydrogen gas in compartment B acts as a reducing agent.
(3) The equation for the overall reaction is:  \( H^{+}(aq) + OH^{-}(aq) \rightarrow H_{2}O(l) \)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
1st statement : To completely neutralise 1 mole of HCl(aq), the number of moles of  \( NH_{3}(aq) \)  needed is more than the number of moles of KOH(aq) needed.
2nd statement : \(NH_{3}(aq)\) is a weaker alkali than KOH(aq).

options.label: A
options.text: Both statements are true and the 2nd statement is a correct explanation of the 1st statement.

options.label: B
options.text: Both statements are true but the 2nd statement is NOT a correct explanation of the 1st statement.

options.label: C
options.text: The 1st statement is false but the 2nd statement is true.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
100 cm \( ^{3} \)  of 1.0 M HCl(aq) reacts with excess zinc granules giving curve A in the graph below.
[IMG:0.jpg]

Which of the following changes may give curve B?

options.label: B
options.text: Use the same mass of zinc powder instead of zinc granules.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Consider the following equilibrium system in a certain liquid medium at  \( 25^{\circ} \) C :
\(  CH_{3}COCH_{3}+HCN\rightleftharpoons(CH_{3})_{2}C(OH)CN\quad\Delta H>0 \)

Which of the following statements is correct (assuming the total volume of the system remains unchanged)?

options.label: A
options.text: Adding  \( (\mathrm{CH}_{3})_{2}\mathrm{C}(\mathrm{OH})\mathrm{CN} \)  would increase the equilibrium constant  \( K_{c} \) .

options.label: B
options.text: Increasing the temperature would increase the concentration of  \( (\mathrm{CH}_{3})_{2}\mathrm{C}(\mathrm{OH})\mathrm{CN} \) .

options.label: C
options.text: The concentration of  \( CH_{3}COCH_{3} \)  must be equal to the concentration of  \( (\mathrm{CH}_{3})_{2}\mathrm{C}(\mathrm{CH}_{3})\mathrm{CN} \) .

options.label: D
options.text: After adding HCN and when a new equilibrium is attained, the concentration of HCN would be restored to the value before the addition of HCN.

questions.question_text: Which of the following statements is correct?

options.label: A
options.text: The boiling point of argon is lower than that of neon.

options.label: B
options.text: The boiling point of nitrogen is lower than that of oxygen.

options.label: C
options.text: The melting point of silicon is lower than that of sodium.

options.label: D
options.text: The melting point of aluminium is lower than that of magnesium.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
The equilibrium constant  \( K_{c} \)  for the reaction  \( \mathrm{N}_{2}\mathrm{O}_{4}(\mathrm{~g}) \rightleftharpoons 2\mathrm{NO}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \)  at  \( 70^{\circ}C \)  is  \( 0.13\ mol\ dm^{-3} \) . In a  \( 5.0\ dm^{3} \)  closed container kept at  \( 70^{\circ}C \) , there is a mixture of  \( 0.20\ mol \)  of  \( \mathrm{N}_{2}\mathrm{O}_{4}(\mathrm{~g}) \)  and  \( 0.30\ mol \)  of  \( \mathrm{NO}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \)  at a certain moment. Which of the following combinations is correct at that moment?
Reaction quotient  \( Q_{c} \)  / mol dm \( ^{-3} \)   |    Rate of the reaction

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Consider the following conversion :
[IMG:0.jpg]   \(\xrightarrow{\text{Step(I)}}\)   intermediate compound   \(\xrightarrow{\text{Step(II)}}\)   [IMG:1.jpg]

Which of the following combinations can achieve the above conversion?

Reagent used in Step (I)    |    Reagent used in Step (II)

options.label: B
options.text: aqueous potassium hydroxide    |    dilute sulphuric acid

options.label: D
options.text: aqueous potassium hydroxide    |    concentrated sulphuric acid

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Which of the following compounds CANNOT form condensation polymers?

(1)  \( H_{2}N(CH_{2})_{5}CO_{2}H \)
(2)  \( CH_{3}CO_{2}CH=CH_{2} \)
(3)  \( \mathrm{CH_{3}CH(OH)CO_{2}H} \)

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 12: Patterns in the Chemical World
questions.question_text:
Which of the following processes can illustrate the characteristics of transition metals?

(1) mixing  \( AgNO_{3}(aq) \)  and NaCl(aq)
(2) mixing  \( FeSO_{4}(aq) \)  and  \( Br_{2}(aq) \)
(3) mixing  \( \mathrm{CuSO_{4}(s)} \)  and  \( \mathrm{H_{2}O(l)} \)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Consider the following two reactions :
ReactionReactants
(I)1.0 g of \( \mathrm{Na}_{2}\mathrm{CO}_{3}(\mathrm{s}) \) + 100.0 cm \( ^{3} \) of 1.0 M HCl(aq)
(II)1.0 g of \( \mathrm{Na}_{2}\mathrm{SO}_{4}(\mathrm{s}) \) + 100.0 cm \( ^{3} \) of l.0 M \( \mathrm{CH}_{3}\mathrm{COOH}(\mathrm{aq}) \)
Which of the following statements are correct if the two reactions are performed under the same experimental conditions? (Relative atomic masses : C = 12.0, O = 16.0, Na = 23.0) (1) The decrease in mass for the two reaction mixtures is the same. (2) The initial rate of Reaction (I) is higher than that of Reaction (II). (3) The heat given out for the two reactions is the same.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Which of the following statements concerning soap are correct?

(1) Soap is an ester.
(2) Soap can reduce the surface tension of water.
(3) Soap particles consist of both hydrophobic and hydrophilic parts.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
An organic compound has the following structure :
[IMG:0.jpg]
Which of the following statements concerning this compound are correct?

(1) It has an ester group.
(2) It contains at least one chiral centre.
(3) It reacts with acidified sodium dichromate solution to form a ketone.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
1st statement : The molar volume of bromine is larger than that of fluorine at room temperature and pressure.
2nd statement : The molecular size of bromine is larger than that of fluorine.

options.label: A
options.text: Both statements are true and the 2nd statement is a correct explanation of the 1st statement.

options.label: B
options.text: Both statements are true but the 2nd statement is NOT a correct explanation of the 1st statement.

options.label: C
options.text: The 1st statement is false but the 2nd statement is true.
